message:
feat: add sort, topk, where, linear algebra ops, tensor manipulation, and loss functions

This commit adds a substantial set of new operations across all four core
crates (ft-api, ft-autograd, ft-dispatch, ft-kernel-cpu), bringing
FrankenTorch closer to PyTorch API parity for common tensor operations.
The additions span six major categories:

--- Sorting and selection operations ---

- sort(): stable sort along a given dimension (ascending/descending) with
  full autograd support. Returns sorted values tensor and original indices.
  Backward pass scatters upstream gradients back to original positions
  using the sort permutation.
- topk(): return the k largest or smallest elements along a dimension.
  Supports sorted output and largest/smallest selection. Backward pass
  scatters gradients to the original k positions via stored indices.
- Both operations include dispatch routing (SortDispatchDecision,
  TopKDispatchDecision) and CPU kernel implementations with dimension-
  strided iteration for N-D tensors.

--- Conditional selection ---

- where(condition, x, y): element-wise conditional selection matching
  PyTorch's torch.where(). Selects from x where condition is non-zero,
  from y otherwise. Backward pass routes gradients to x or y based on
  the condition mask (condition itself receives no gradient).

--- Linear algebra operations ---

- dot(): inner product of two 1-D tensors. Backward: grad_lhs = grad * rhs,
  grad_rhs = grad * lhs.
- outer(): outer product of two 1-D vectors producing a 2-D matrix.
  Backward uses the cross-vector to reconstruct input gradients.
- bmm(): batched matrix multiplication for 3-D tensors [B, M, K] x [B, K, N].
  Backward: grad_lhs = grad @ rhs^T, grad_rhs = lhs^T @ grad (per batch).
- trace(): sum of diagonal elements of a 2-D matrix. Backward places
  upstream gradient on the diagonal of a zeros matrix matching input shape.

--- Tensor creation and manipulation ---

- eye(n): identity matrix creation (n x n).
- diag(): dual-purpose diagonal operation — creates a diagonal matrix from
  a 1-D tensor, or extracts the diagonal from a 2-D tensor.
- triu(k) / tril(k): upper and lower triangular extraction with diagonal
  offset parameter, matching PyTorch semantics.
- linspace(start, end, steps): evenly spaced values (inclusive endpoints).
- full_like / zeros_like / ones_like: shape-preserving tensor factories.
- flip(dims): reverse elements along specified dimensions with autograd
  (backward is flip along the same dims).
- repeat(repeats): tile a tensor along each dimension with autograd
  (backward sums repeated gradient blocks back to original shape).
- roll(shift, dim): circular shift along a dimension with autograd
  (backward rolls in the opposite direction).

--- Tensor introspection utilities ---

- tensor_shape() promoted from private to public API.
- tensor_dim(): number of dimensions.
- tensor_numel(): total element count.
- tensor_item(): extract scalar from single-element tensor.
- tensor_clone(): deep copy detached from computation graph.
- tensor_detach(): copy with requires_grad=false.
- tensor_any() / tensor_all(): boolean reduction predicates.
- tensor_median(): median value (lower-of-two for even counts, matching
  PyTorch integer-like behavior).

--- Loss functions ---

- nll_loss(): negative log likelihood loss for classification. Expects
  log-probabilities [batch, classes] and integer targets [batch].
  Returns mean NLL loss.
- cross_entropy_loss(): combined log_softmax + nll_loss, equivalent to
  PyTorch's F.cross_entropy(). Applies log-softmax along the last
  dimension before computing NLL.

--- Autograd graph integration ---

All new operations that support gradients are fully wired into the
TensorNodeOp enum, the backward pass dispatcher, and the DAC operation
event recording system. New operation events (TensorSortOperationEvent,
TensorTopKOperationEvent) and their recording functions are added for
deterministic replay. The existing dependency-tracking arms in the
backward pass (input enumeration, requires_grad propagation) are extended
to cover Dot, Outer, Bmm, Trace, Where, Sort, TopK, Flip, Repeat, and
Roll operations.

--- Test coverage ---

~90 new unit tests across ft-kernel-cpu (20 tests) and ft-api (70 tests)
covering:
- Forward correctness for all new operations (1-D, 2-D, N-D cases)
- Backward pass / gradient correctness for sort, topk, where, dot, outer,
  bmm, trace, flip, repeat, roll, cumsum, cumprod
- Edge cases: invalid dimensions, k exceeding dimension size, empty
  tensors, single-element tensors, rectangular matrices
- Loss function sanity: cross-entropy ordering (correct class < wrong class)
- Introspection utilities: shape, dim, numel, item, clone, detach, any,
  all, median

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

+// --- Sort dispatch ---
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
+pub struct SortDispatchDecision {
+    pub dim: usize,
+    pub descending: bool,
+    pub mode: ExecutionMode,
+    pub kernel: &'static str,
+    pub selected_key: DispatchKey,
+    pub backend_key: DispatchKey,
+    pub keyset_bits: u64,
+    pub fallback_used: bool,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
+pub struct TensorSortDispatchOutcome {
+    pub values: Vec<f64>,
+    pub indices: Vec<usize>,
+    pub decision: SortDispatchDecision,
+}
+
+pub fn dispatch_tensor_sort_contiguous_f64(
+    mode: ExecutionMode,
+    input: &[f64],
+    meta: &TensorMeta,
+    dim: usize,
+    descending: bool,
+    requires_grad: bool,
+) -> Result<TensorSortDispatchOutcome, DispatchError> {
+    let keyset = dispatch_keyset_for_single_tensor_meta(meta, requires_grad);
+    let (selected_key, backend_key, effective_key, fallback_used) =
+        resolve_dispatch_keys(mode, keyset)?;
+
+    let ((values, indices), kernel) = match effective_key {
+        DispatchKey::AutogradCPU => (
+            sort_tensor_contiguous_f64(input, meta, dim, descending)?,
+            "autograd_cpu::sort_tensor_contiguous_f64",
+        ),
+        DispatchKey::CPU => (
+            sort_tensor_contiguous_f64(input, meta, dim, descending)?,
+            "cpu::sort_tensor_contiguous_f64",
+        ),
+        _ => {
+            return Err(DispatchKeyError::IncompatibleSet {
+                reason: "resolved dispatch key is unsupported for contiguous tensor sort op",
+            }
+            .into());
+        }
+    };
+
+    Ok(TensorSortDispatchOutcome {
+        values,
+        indices,
+        decision: SortDispatchDecision {
+            dim,
+            descending,
+            mode,
+            kernel,
+            selected_key,
+            backend_key,
+            keyset_bits: keyset.bits(),
+            fallback_used,
+        },
+    })
+}
+
+// --- TopK dispatch ---
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
+pub struct TopKDispatchDecision {
+    pub k: usize,
+    pub dim: usize,
+    pub largest: bool,
+    pub sorted: bool,
+    pub mode: ExecutionMode,
+    pub kernel: &'static str,
+    pub selected_key: DispatchKey,
+    pub backend_key: DispatchKey,
+    pub keyset_bits: u64,
+    pub fallback_used: bool,
+}
+
+#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
+pub struct TensorTopKDispatchOutcome {
+    pub values: Vec<f64>,
+    pub indices: Vec<usize>,
+    pub decision: TopKDispatchDecision,
+}
+
+#[allow(clippy::too_many_arguments)]
+pub fn dispatch_tensor_topk_contiguous_f64(
+    mode: ExecutionMode,
+    input: &[f64],
+    meta: &TensorMeta,
+    k: usize,
+    dim: usize,
+    largest: bool,
+    sorted: bool,
+    requires_grad: bool,
+) -> Result<TensorTopKDispatchOutcome, DispatchError> {
+    let keyset = dispatch_keyset_for_single_tensor_meta(meta, requires_grad);
+    let (selected_key, backend_key, effective_key, fallback_used) =
+        resolve_dispatch_keys(mode, keyset)?;
+
+    let ((values, indices), kernel) = match effective_key {
+        DispatchKey::AutogradCPU => (
+            topk_tensor_contiguous_f64(input, meta, k, dim, largest, sorted)?,
+            "autograd_cpu::topk_tensor_contiguous_f64",
+        ),
+        DispatchKey::CPU => (
+            topk_tensor_contiguous_f64(input, meta, k, dim, largest, sorted)?,
+            "cpu::topk_tensor_contiguous_f64",
+        ),
+        _ => {
+            return Err(DispatchKeyError::IncompatibleSet {
+                reason: "resolved dispatch key is unsupported for contiguous tensor topk op",
+            }
+            .into());
+        }
+    };
+
+    Ok(TensorTopKDispatchOutcome {
+        values,
+        indices,
+        decision: TopKDispatchDecision {
+            k,
+            dim,
+            largest,
+            sorted,
+            mode,
+            kernel,
+            selected_key,
+            backend_key,
+            keyset_bits: keyset.bits(),
+            fallback_used,
+        },
+    })
+}
+
 #[cfg(test)]
 mod tests {
     use std::collections::BTreeMap;
